errors: Answer says effective October 1, 2011 for Title 32 AGR qualifying service; source confirms this date. Answer also states retroactive payment possible for enrollments on or after August 1, 2009 — this is an additional claim not directly verifiable from the portion of M22-4 read; plausible but unconfirmed detail.

Claim that bartending courses are 'always prohibited under all VA education benefit chapters' is directionally correct per the restrictions chapter keyword metadata; source article not fully readable to confirm exact statutory basis, but bartending prohibition is a well-established VA training restriction. Minor confidence gap due to unread source.

The nuanced claim that private pilot certificate courses 'may be taken at an IHL if it is for credit toward a college degree' is plausible per known VA policy but could not be fully verified from the unread restrictions article. Core policy (vocational flight for private pilot = prohibited) is correct.

The specific claim of 'second-class medical certificate, valid for 12 months' is a known FAA/VA policy detail. Source article 554400000073476 was not read; cannot verify 100%. The answer is consistent with known VA education flight training policy.

Answer correctly states Chapter 35 and Section 901 beneficiaries are NOT eligible for vocational flight training. Source article was not read; cannot fully confirm the specific mention of 'Section 901' in the source, though policy is well-established.

Answer '9 credits' for 3/4-time when full time is 12 credits is standard per standard VA credit hour tables. Source article 554400000073534 was not directly read; answer is consistent with known VA measurement policy.

The answer '3/4 time (10-12 credits = 3/4 time when full time is 13+)' is consistent with VA measurement tables. Source not directly read. The table ranges for nonstandard terms (13+ credit full time) cannot be 100% confirmed without reading source.
Answer 'Code 4' for Chapter 1606 full-time BDN training time code. Source not directly read. This is a BDN-specific technical code and could not be verified against the source article.

Answer states interval payments are no longer payable as of August 1, 2011 per Section 206, PL 111-377. Source article 554400000073579 was not read. The PL 111-377 reference and August 1, 2011 date are consistent with other PL 111-377 changes confirmed in the housing allowance article, making this plausible.
Answer states summer-term interval payable when at least 8 weeks long. Source article 554400000073579 was not read; cannot verify the 8-week threshold from source.

The 60-day suspense for EPC 270 (vs. 30-day standard) is stated as policy in query 103's answer and implied in query 84 within the golden set itself. Source article 554400000073486 was not fully read to the EPC suspense section; the 60-day claim is internally consistent across multiple queries in the golden set.

The answer '60% of established tuition and fees for one term' is stated as the Chapter 30 accelerated payment cap. Source article 554400000076342 was not read; cannot verify from source. The 60% figure is a known VA policy, but unconfirmed against actual source text.

EPC 240 for original Chapter 35 claim. Source article 554400000061193 (M22-3 Appendix A) could not be read due to token limits. EPC 240 is a plausible answer for Chapter 35 original claims but cannot be confirmed against the actual EPC table.

EPC 280 for Chapter 1606 supplemental. Source article 554400000061193 could not be read. EPC 280 is consistent with the naming convention pattern (200=Ch30, 220=Ch32, 240=Ch35, 260=Ch1606 original; supplemental EPCs use different codes) but cannot be confirmed without reading the source.

October 1, 2023 as the SOAR/SAO transition date. The portion of M22-3 Chapter 2 (article 554400000059332) read did not include the specific SOAR transition date. The date appears internally consistent in query 80's answer and query 94. Cannot fully confirm from source text read.

Answer states Chapter 32 delimiting date is 10 years from discharge. Source article 554400000073604 was partially accessible via grep. The 10-year figure is consistent with known Chapter 32 policy (vs. 15 years for Chapter 33 pre-2013). Cannot fully verify the exact wording from source.

Answer references the 'CAVC decision in Ozer v. Principi' for Chapter 35 surviving spouses having no delimiting date, and PL 107-103 effective December 28, 2001 for restoring it to regular Chapter 35 spouses. Source article could not be fully read. The legal citations are specific and internally plausible but unverified against source text.

The specific claim about '$1,200 MGIB refund (96R) payable under Chapter 33' when entitlement exhausts. Source article 554400000073421 was not read; this is consistent with what query 67 also describes, and is a well-known CH33 provision.

Transaction code '06L' for work study allowance in Benefits Manager. Source article 554400000289086 was not read. This is a specific BDN transaction code and cannot be confirmed without reading the source.

July 8, 2024 as the BDN-to-eMPWR transition date for Chapter 33 payment review. This date appears consistently across multiple queries (47, 75, 84, 90, 95, 97, 99) in the golden set. Source article 554400000291771 was not directly read, but the date is highly consistent internally.
'15 minutes of inactivity' for DGI record lock release. Source article 554400000303799 was not read. This detail appears again in queries 81, 86, and 95, internally consistent across the golden set.

PL 116-315 Section 1010, Phase 1 NCD August 1, 2021, Phase 2 IHL December 17, 2021 for two-certification requirement. Source article 554400000191366 was not read. These specific dates and phases appear consistently across queries 49, 99, 101, 108. Cannot directly verify from source but internally highly consistent.

Answer states codes A, B, H, and J qualify for Chapter 33. VADIR Table 42 confirmed H = 'Under honorable conditions (absence of a negative report)' and J = 'Honorable for VA Purposes.' The answer's claim that all four codes qualify for Chapter 33 is directionally correct, but M22-4 eligibility rules for character of service were not verified chapter-and-verse. The pairing of Table 42 data with M22-4 eligibility determination is a cross-source synthesis that is directionally accurate.

Answer cites Table 25 for trainingPeriod data element (beginDate, endDate, projectCode). This is consistent with the document structure, but Table 25 was not directly verified in the VADIR ICD pages read (read covered Tables 20-24 in detail and referenced others). The claim about Army BCT + AIT as an example of entry-level training is confirmed in the excluded service article. The 730-day threshold for training deduction is confirmed from the M22-4 benefit level article.

Answer references 'mgibCd' and 'mgibSeparationCode' as field names in VADIR Table 23. Table 23 (ServicePeriod Data Element) was not fully read for all field names — the pages covering Table 23 detail were pages 16-20 of the ICD, which were read but mgibSeparationCode is not confirmed as the exact field name. Minor uncertainty on exact field nomenclature.

Answer describes 'militaryStatus is a two-digit code (01-16) denoting duty status' and 'activeServiceIndicator' field. The exact militaryStatus field range (01-16) and the activeServiceIndicator field name were not directly confirmed from the ICD pages read. The housing allowance policy (veteran not on active duty + enrolled above half-time = MHA eligible) is confirmed from the housing allowance article.

The answer claims 'There is no specific EPC for Chapter 33 in M22-3 Appendix A.' This is stated as a fact, but the EPC table (article 554400000061193) could not be read due to token limits. This is a potentially incorrect claim — it's plausible that Chapter 33 LTS handles processing without traditional EPCs, but if the EPC table actually contains a Chapter 33 EPC, the answer would be factually wrong on the core claim. Additionally, the answer states EPCs 200/220/240/260 cover Chapters 30, 32, 35, and 1606 respectively — these specific mappings cannot be verified from the unread source. High uncertainty on the core factual claims.

Answer states eduPgmEntlMtsQy = 24 means 24 months remaining. Per Table 30, eduPgmEntlMtsQy is 'Numeric value 0 to 36' and is the entitlement months quantity. The answer's interpretation (24 remaining = 12 used) is a reasonable inference. The answer also states eduPgmTypCd '2' maps to a program type — Table 30 was confirmed to have values '1','2','4','5' for eduPgmTypCd, consistent with the answer.

Answer cites '(1) activePeriod's pgibCd and pgibSeparationCode (Table 23)' and '(2) MGIB status codes mgad_loss_cat_codes (Table 48) where code 01 = Service connected disability.' Table 48 was confirmed in the ICD pages read (pages 26-31) as MGIB Status codes, but the specific field name 'mgad_loss_cat_codes' and code '01 = Service connected disability' were not confirmed from Table 48 text. Table 48 covered MGIB Status codes for Chapter 30 MGIB eligibility, not specifically loss categories. The answer also references 'Table 52' for PGIB codes — Table 52 was not confirmed in the ICD pages read (pages ended at Table 51). These specific table references and code values are unverified.

Answer describes the DGI fallback workflow in detail including 'Create New Chapter 33 Record or perform a New Search.' Source article 554400000303799 (DGI processing) was not read; the DGI workflow steps are internally consistent with other DGI references in the golden set.

Answer describes resultsHash as 'SHA-256 hash of legacy and new values.' Per the ICD Table 22 (verified), resultsHash is 'a digital fingerprint' in the format 'sha 256 hash of legacy values and sha 256 hash of new values with a dash char in the middle' — it is two separate SHA-256 hashes combined, not a single SHA-256 hash. The answer simplifies this as 'SHA-256 hash' which is slightly imprecise. Minor technical imprecision.

Answer references 'M22-4 Part 4 Chapter 6 Section 6.06' and 'Chapter 14, Tables 14.02a-d.' These specific section numbers were not verified from the unread source article. The overall approach (converting to credit-hour equivalents, using measurement tables) is consistent with known VA measurement procedures.

The Chapter 1606 big-pay thresholds ($8,000-$11,999.99 for three signatures; $12,000+ for four) were not confirmed from the source — the administrative issues article (554400000073486) was read to around line 929 which confirmed Chapter 33 thresholds but did not clearly reach the Chapter 1606 row. These specific dollar amounts are plausible given the pattern (Chapter 33 school at $45K/beneficiary at $25K; Chapter 30/35 at $20K/$25K), but the Chapter 1606 row values are unverified.

Answer references VADIR Section 6.1.11 (PGIB Codes) and Table 52. Table 52 and Section 6.1.11 were not encountered in the ICD pages read (read covered through page 33 which ended at Table 51 MGSR codes). Cannot verify these specific references.

Answer mentions 'project code A26' for AGR in Table 43. Table 43 (Project Codes) was read and confirmed 9GY and 9GF, but did not explicitly read every project code; A26 as specifically an AGR code was not confirmed. The July 8, 2024 eMPWR transition date and statute codes N/P/Q from Table 44 are confirmed correct.

Answer states 'Error codes 16820002-16820005 indicate VADIR-side failures with no automated retry.' Confirmed from Table 41: 16820001 = Invalid Search Criteria, 16820004 = Error during transformation. The range 16820002-16820005 is consistent with the error code series shown in Table 41, but not all codes in the range were explicitly read. Minor uncertainty on exact error code range boundaries.

Answer states QA scorecard has '13 questions across Data Integrity (Q1-3), Development (Q4-5), Eligibility (Q6), Entitlement (Q7), Payment (Q8-10), and Notification (Q11-13).' The article reading confirmed the scorecard has 16 questions across 7 categories (Q1-3 Data Integrity, Q4-5 Development, Q6 Eligibility, Q7 Entitlement, Q8-10 Payment, Q11-13 Notification, Q14-16 Systems Compliance). The answer omits Systems Compliance questions Q14-16 and says '13 questions' instead of 16. This is an error — the QA scorecard has 16 questions, not 13.

Answer references '48-month rule (M22-4) caps combined entitlement across programs.' This is consistent with the 48-month rule addressed in query 96. The specific claim about VADIR's bdnEnt tracking 'no cross-program balance' is an analytical assertion about system behavior, not directly verified from source text.

Answer references 'Tables 4-6 (getPerson)' and 'Tables 20-23 (getServicePeriods).' These table number ranges are consistent with the document structure observed but Table 4-6 specifically was not read in detail. The DGI fallback workflow (create new record if VADIR returns no data) references M22-4 Part II Chapter 2 Section D which was not directly read.

Answer references eMPWR 'Transaction History tab (All Transactions, Deductions, Payments, Receivables views).' These specific tab names within eMPWR are not verified from source. The core claim about the July 8, 2024 boundary and BDN still viewable for historical records is consistent with all other references to this transition.

Answer references 'Sections 5.1.7-5.1.8' for response structure. These specific subsection numbers (5.1.7-5.1.8) were not encountered in the ICD pages read (Section 5.1 confirmed as production endpoint). Otherwise the analytical reasoning about payload size and SLA impact is sound.

Answer references M22-4 Part 4 Chapter 6 'E01/E04 screens showing approval status' and OLAF. These specific screen codes and OLAF reference were not verified from source. The escalation path to 'RPO Support Services Division (SSD)' is plausible but unverified.

Answer references 'BT ordering system (overpayments transmitted first, then payments).' This specific sequencing rule was not verified from M22-4 Part II Chapter 1 Section C source. The rest of the answer (eMPWR maintenance impact, DGI independence, record lock behavior) is consistent with verified facts.

Answer describes the 48-month combined entitlement rule under 38 U.S.C. § 3695 and the note that the 48-month cap does NOT apply to Chapter 1607 + National Call to Service combinations. Source articles for Chapter 11 (M22-4 Part 11) were not read; the 48-month rule framework is internally consistent with query 50's answer, but the specific NCS exception cannot be confirmed.

Answer states veterans discharged before January 1, 2013 had a 15-year delimiting date (confirmed from M22-4 Part 12 Sub-Chapter 2). The specific date example (March 15, 2011 → March 15, 2026) is a correct mathematical application. Reference to M22-4 Figure 4.01 for extension types is consistent with the Figure 4.01 (chart for extension types) referenced in the delimiting date article via grep results.

The claim that initial certification can be submitted 'up to 180 days before term start' and second certification 'within 30 days after drop-add period ends, or 60 days from term start, whichever comes first' — these specific windows are plausible per PL 116-315 two-certification policy but source article 554400000191366 was not read to verify exact timing windows.

Answer mentions 'FOCAS system (Flight, On-the-Job Training, Correspondence, and Apprentice System)' for correspondence training. This specific acronym was not verified from source. The claims about no MHA for correspondence-only are confirmed (housing allowance article confirms MHA requires more than half-time in-residence enrollment). The 'at least 50% of students must require 6+ months to complete' rule and the February 2, 1995 prohibition of nonaccredited correspondence are specific claims that could not be verified from unread source.

Answer mentions VET TEC as 'a separate program specifically designed for high-tech training including coding bootcamps — it provides Chapter 33-level MHA but operates under different approval and enrollment rules.' VET TEC reference cannot be fully verified from the portions of source articles read, though the SAA-approval requirement for standard Chapter 33 training is confirmed from the restrictions article keyword metadata.

Answer lists character of service codes including 'Y (Uncharacterized).' Per VADIR Table 42 as read, the codes confirmed are A, B, D, F, H, J, Z. Code Y as 'Uncharacterized' was not explicitly listed in Table 42 in the pages read. This may be an additional code not captured in the portion of Table 42 read. Minor uncertainty.

Answer mentions 'SHARE (Corporate Inquiries, Corporate Update, BIRLS Update)' and 'MPI Toolkit' as systems a VCE needs. These specific system names are referenced in query 92's answer as 'data remediation POC using MPI Toolkit, SHARE, eFolder, TIMS' — consistent internal reference. Source not directly verified but internally consistent across multiple queries.

key_confirmed_facts: VADIR Table 3: Five operations (getPerson, getContactInfo, findPeopleByCriteria, getServicePeriods, getKickerInfo)
VADIR Section 2.8: 2-second SLA for payload return
VADIR Section 2.9: Confidentiality High, Integrity High, Availability Moderate
VADIR Table 41: Error 16820001 = Invalid Search Criteria; Error 16820004 = Transformation error
VADIR Table 42: H = Under honorable conditions; J = Honorable for VA Purposes; Z = Unknown
VADIR Table 43: 9GY = Hurricane Katrina Aug 31 2005 Training N; 9GF = OCO Training N
VADIR Table 45: S = Space Force; F = Air Force
VADIR Table 46: B1 = $28,500 Active Duty Kicker OBSOLETE as of 20041001
VADIR Table 49: Code 51 = Baccalaureate
VADIR Table 50: Code 03 = Participant
VADIR Table 51: CA = Suspended baccalaureate; BA = Eligible initial period; DA = Terminated failed reaffiliate
VADIR Table 22: resultsHash = two SHA-256 hashes combined with dash separator
VADIR Table 24: exclusionPerdTyp = ROTC or APPELLATE
VADIR Table 28: purpleHeartIndCd = Y if Purple Heart evidence
VADIR Table 30: eduPgmEntlMtsQy = numeric 0-36 months; eduPgmTypCd values 1,2,4,5
VADIR Section 5.1: Production endpoint = https://vavdrappprd.va.gov/vdrCH33/v2/VSCH33Service?WSDL
VADIR Section 6.1.2: Blank/unknown character of service defaults to honorable (lone exception)
M22-4 70/80 rule: 730-909 days including training AND 545-729 excluding = 70%
M22-4: 40% benefit level eliminated August 1, 2020
M22-4: PL 115-48 — no delimiting date for veterans discharged on/after January 1, 2013
M22-4: ROTC excluded period = 4 years; Service Academy = 5 years
M22-4: Texas → Muskogee RPO; Foreign schools → Buffalo RPO
M22-4: Chapter 33 big pay — $45K school / $25K beneficiary for four signatures
M22-4: DEA folder = blue two-part
M22-4: MHA for distance learning = 50% of national average BAH, effective October 1, 2011
M22-4: OJT housing — 100%/80%/60%/40%/20% in sequential 6-month periods
M22-4: Part-time student at 9 credits (12 = FT) → rate of pursuit 0.75 → 80% BAH
M22-3: QA scorecard has 16 questions across 7 categories (Q1-3 Data Integrity, Q4-5 Development, Q6 Eligibility, Q7 Entitlement, Q8-10 Payment, Q11-13 Notification, Q14-16 Systems Compliance)
confirmed_errors: Query 80: Answer states QA scorecard has 13 questions; actual count is 16 (Q1-Q16 across 7 categories including Q14-16 Systems Compliance). Score reduced to 0.9.
unverified_but_plausible: Query 62: Claim that no EPC exists for Chapter 33 in M22-3 Appendix A — unread source, score 0.7 due to high uncertainty on core assertion.
Query 65: Table 48 code '01 = Service connected disability' and mgad_loss_cat_codes field name — unverified.
Queries 24, 25: EPC 240/280 values — EPC source article unreadable.
Queries 11-17, 22, 42-49: Scores of 0.9 reflecting source articles not read.
